_meta.description: Curated sub-national malaria decision-support dataset for Goodbye Malaria field operations in Mozambique and Malawi. Values are synthesized from open sources for hackathon/demo use and should be re-validated against live feeds before operational use. Geo/elevation fields are approximate, curated for thematic mapping and nearest-region matching.
sources: WHO World Malaria Report 2025 (district incidence bands)
Malaria Atlas Project (MAP) sub-national PfPR estimates
CHIRPS daily rainfall (UCSB Climate Hazards Center)
SRTM / Copernicus GLO-30 DEM + HAND (low-lying / flood-prone zones)
PMI/Goodbye Malaria IRS + ITN campaign records
WHO Global database on insecticide resistance
_meta.season_note: Southern Africa transmission is rainfall-driven. Core wet season is roughly Nov-Apr; June is normally dry-season trough. Early/anomalous rains shift risk forward. 2025-2026 saw an anomalous early-rain surge in parts of southern Mozambique.
_meta.elevation_note: Low-lying, flat ground near rivers/lakes pools water after rain and becomes larval habitat within days. Elevation + flood-proneness are first-order signals for where to target larviciding and larval source management.

gaza.incidence_trend: High and rising. WHO WMR 2025 places Gaza districts in the high-burden band (>450 cases/1000/yr in Chókwè/Guijá irrigated zones). 3-year trend upward, driven by Limpopo valley irrigation and flood cycles.

current_status.headline: Active flood-driven case surge in the Chókwè–Guijá irrigation zone after the 2025–26 anomalous early-rain surge; extensive new standing water.
current_status.trend: High-burden band (>450 cases/1,000/yr in irrigated districts), rising over the past ~3 weeks.
current_status.source: WHO WMR 2025 district bands + CHIRPS rainfall anomaly

gaza.rainfall_flood_status: CHIRPS flagged an ANOMALOUS EARLY-RAIN SURGE across southern Mozambique in late 2025 / early 2026 — 150-200% of normal for the period, with localized flooding along the Limpopo. Extensive standing water and new breeding habitat in Chókwè and Guijá.
gaza.season: Normal wet season Nov-Mar; 2026 onset was early. Flood-associated transmission spikes follow standing water by 2-4 weeks.
gaza.elevation_flood: Limpopo valley floor 10-40 m — flat alluvial irrigation plain (Chókwè scheme), strongly low-lying. Flood-proneness VERY HIGH; the 2026 early-rain surge pooled extensively across the irrigation plain and Limpopo floodplain near Xai-Xai.
gaza.last_intervention: IRS completed Oct 2025 (Actellic 300CS) ahead of normal season; ITN mass campaign 2024 (PermaNet 3.0), coverage now decaying (~55% usable). No larviciding program currently active.
gaza.resistance_profile: Confirmed pyrethroid resistance (deltamethrin, permethrin) in An. funestus. Organophosphate (pirimiphos-methyl / Actellic) and clothianidin (SumiShield) still effective. Avoid pyrethroid-only tools as primary vector control.
gaza.infrastructure: Irrigated agricultural zone, peri-urban Xai-Xai and Chókwè town. Road access good in dry conditions but flood-disrupted. Spray teams and Bti stocks reachable from provincial depot.

low_lying_zones.name: Chókwè irrigation plain
low_lying_zones.note: Flat alluvial scheme, <40 m, pools rapidly

maputo.incidence_trend: Moderate and declining in the city; higher in Manhiça/Magude rural districts. Maputo is the national elimination-frontier province (8 southern districts targeting elimination).

current_status.headline: Dry-season trough; city transmission low and declining. Elimination-corridor surveillance ongoing in Magude/Manhiça.
current_status.trend: Moderate and declining in the city; focal rural risk only.
current_status.source: WHO WMR 2025 + Goodbye Malaria elimination programme

maputo.rainfall_flood_status: Early 2026 rains above normal but less extreme than Gaza. Localized standing water in peri-urban Matola.
maputo.elevation_flood: Coastal lowland 5-50 m. Manhiça/Incomáti floodplain low-lying. Flood-proneness MODERATE; localized peri-urban pooling in Matola wetlands.
maputo.last_intervention: IRS in elimination districts 2025 (SumiShield / clothianidin); reactive case-based surveillance and focal response active in Magude/Manhiça (Goodbye Malaria elimination corridor).
maputo.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance present; clothianidin and pirimiphos-methyl effective. Elimination strategy favors IRS + surveillance over mass ITN.
maputo.infrastructure: Strong — capital region, best logistics in country. Good for rapid response and case investigation.

sofala.incidence_trend: Very high. Central Mozambique holds the largest national burden. Cyclone-prone coast (Beira) with recurrent flood-driven epidemics.

current_status.headline: Beira flood corridor on alert; persistent standing water from Búzi/Pungwe floodplains keeps breeding pressure high even in dry season.
current_status.trend: Very high year-round; acute cyclone/flood spikes.

sofala.rainfall_flood_status: Cyclone/flood corridor. Post-cyclone standing water repeatedly triggers larviciding needs. 2026 early season wet.
sofala.season: Wet season Nov-Apr; cyclone season Dec-Mar adds acute flood spikes.
sofala.elevation_flood: Beira sits at/near sea level — much of the city is below 5 m and among the most flood-exposed cities in Africa. Búzi and Pungwe floodplains very low. Flood-proneness VERY HIGH; storm-surge + river flooding leave persistent standing water.
sofala.last_intervention: ITN mass campaign 2024 (Interceptor G2 — chlorfenapyr+pyrethroid, chosen for resistance). IRS focal in Beira peri-urban.
sofala.resistance_profile: Strong pyrethroid resistance — this is why Interceptor G2 (dual-active, chlorfenapyr) was deployed. ATSB and OP-based IRS are resistance-robust options.
sofala.infrastructure: Beira port logistics good; rural Búzi/Caia flood-isolated in wet season.

low_lying_zones.name: Beira coastal city (<5 m)
low_lying_zones.note: At/near sea level; drainage canals pool

zambezia.incidence_trend: Among the two highest-burden provinces nationally (with Nampula). High year-round transmission.

current_status.headline: Among the two highest-burden provinces nationally; high transmission persists into the dry season.

zambezia.rainfall_flood_status: High rainfall province; extensive lowland flooding in wet season.
zambezia.elevation_flood: Coastal delta lowland near sea level around Quelimane; Licungo and Zambezi floodplains very low and broad. Flood-proneness HIGH; long wet season sustains lowland standing water.
zambezia.last_intervention: ITN mass campaign 2024 (Interceptor G2). Limited IRS coverage.
zambezia.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance widespread; dual-active nets and OP/clothianidin IRS preferred.
zambezia.infrastructure: Large, populous, mixed access. SMC being scaled in seasonal-transmission districts.

tete.incidence_trend: High in lowland Zambezi valley; Angónia highlands lower and more seasonal.

current_status.headline: Zambezi valley lowlands elevated; Cahora Bassa dam releases sustain downstream standing water. Angónia highlands lower/seasonal.
current_status.trend: High in the valley, lower in the highlands.

tete.rainfall_flood_status: Zambezi valley flood exposure; Cahora Bassa dam releases affect downstream standing water.
tete.elevation_flood: Zambezi valley floor ~100-200 m (hot, low, flood-exposed near Tete/Moatize). Angónia highlands ~1200-1500 m — cooler, lower transmission. Flood-proneness HIGH in valley, LOW in highlands; dam releases raise downstream pooling.
tete.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance present; OP-based IRS effective.
tete.infrastructure: Mining corridor logistics in Moatize; rural valley access seasonal.

nampula.incidence_trend: Highest-burden province nationally alongside Zambézia. Very high, year-round transmission.

current_status.headline: Highest-burden province alongside Zambézia; near year-round transmission with perennial breeding habitat.

nampula.rainfall_flood_status: Coastal/near-coastal high rainfall; perennial breeding habitat.
nampula.season: Wet season Dec-Apr; transmission near year-round.
nampula.elevation_flood: Coastal plain rising to interior plateau (~400 m around Nampula city). Near-coastal low-lying around Angoche and Nacala bay. Flood-proneness MODERATE-HIGH near the coast; perennial breeding habitat regardless.
nampula.last_intervention: ITN mass campaign 2024 (Interceptor G2). EAVE TUBE (In2Care) TRIAL conducted in Nampula — demonstrated 50-70% reduction in mosquito house entry; a candidate for structural scale-up. IRS limited.
nampula.resistance_profile: High pyrethroid resistance. Eave tubes (In2Care, deploys pyrethroid + fungal biopesticide) and ATSB attractive precisely because they bypass pyrethroid-resistance. OP/clothianidin IRS effective.
nampula.infrastructure: Nacala port + corridor; large rural hinterland. Annual entomological monitoring site — resistance data relatively current here.

cabo_delgado.incidence_trend: Very high; humanitarian/conflict disruption to services in northern districts complicates coverage.

current_status.headline: Very high burden; insecurity and IDP movement disrupt services and concentrate risk in the north.

cabo_delgado.rainfall_flood_status: Coastal high rainfall; IDP movement concentrates risk.
cabo_delgado.elevation_flood: Coastal lowland to interior plateau (Montepuez ~500 m). Flood-proneness MODERATE near the coast; risk is driven as much by IDP concentration as by elevation.
cabo_delgado.last_intervention: ITN distribution to IDP/host communities 2024; service continuity fragile in conflict-affected districts.
cabo_delgado.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance present; dual-active nets prioritized.
cabo_delgado.infrastructure: Constrained by insecurity in north; Pemba functional. Plan interventions that tolerate access disruption.

zomba.incidence_trend: High. Southern Malawi (Shire valley + lakeshore) carries the heaviest national burden; Zomba lakeshore and plain are high-transmission.

current_status.headline: Dry-season trough (June); transmission builds from November (Jan–Apr peak). Window for planning, not emergency response.
current_status.trend: High in season; low right now.

zomba.rainfall_flood_status: June is dry-season trough. Rains and transmission build from Nov; peak Jan-Apr.
zomba.season: Wet season Nov-Apr. Transmission peak follows rains by weeks. (If a worker says transmission starts in ~6 weeks from mid-June, that points to an early-season planning window rather than the Nov onset — treat as month-ahead preparation.)
zomba.elevation_flood: Zomba town ~900 m at the foot of the Zomba Massif (peaks ~2000 m). The Lake Chilwa basin to the east is flat lowland ~620 m and flood-prone. Flood-proneness HIGH in the basin/plain, LOW on the plateau — target the lowland, not the massif.
zomba.last_intervention: ITN mass campaign 2024 (national, Royal Guard/Interceptor G2 mix). IRS not routine in Zomba. SMC piloted in southern districts; IPTp routine in ANC.
zomba.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance confirmed in An. funestus across southern Malawi; chlorfenapyr (Interceptor G2) and OP-based IRS effective. PBO and dual-active nets prioritized.
zomba.infrastructure: Reasonable — Zomba town, district health office, ANC network for IPTp; under-5 reach for SMC via health posts.

machinga.incidence_trend: Very high. Lakeshore/Lake Chilwa basin district in southern Malawi; among the highest-burden districts nationally.

current_status.headline: Dry-season trough now; the Lake Chilwa basin becomes prime larval habitat fast once early rains arrive — watch for the first rains.
current_status.trend: Very high in season; low right now.

machinga.rainfall_flood_status: Lake Chilwa basin — flood-prone, abundant standing water when rains arrive. Early rains create extensive larval habitat quickly.
machinga.season: Wet season Nov-Apr; lake-basin flooding amplifies breeding. Early rains => immediate larval-source risk.
machinga.elevation_flood: Low-lying Lake Chilwa basin (~620 m) and Shire valley at Liwonde — flat, broad, abundant standing water when rains arrive. Flood-proneness VERY HIGH; this is prime larval habitat and the reason larviciding leads here.
machinga.last_intervention: ITN mass campaign 2024 (Interceptor G2). IRS not routine. IPTp routine in ANC; SMC in catchment of southern districts.
machinga.resistance_profile: Pyrethroid resistance confirmed (An. funestus). Dual-active nets, larviciding (Bti — biological, no resistance concern), and OP-based IRS effective. Larviciding is resistance-agnostic and ideal for acute flood response.
machinga.infrastructure: Liwonde as district hub; lakeshore communities more dispersed. Bti and larval source management feasible around defined water bodies.
